metadata.notes: Country-level static dataset for first demo web application.

application_fee.notes: Studee notes that a student visa costs SGD 30 processing fee plus SGD 60 issuance fee; total SGD 90.

legal_work_hours_per_week.notes: MOM work pass exemption rules permit Student’s Pass holders to work up to 16 hours per week during term time.

application_fee.notes: Long‑Term Visit Pass (graduate visa) requires SGD 45 application fee plus SGD 60 issuance fee; optional multiple‑journey visa fee excluded.

duration_years.notes: Study abroad sources indicate graduates can stay on the Long‑Term Visit Pass for up to two years to look for employment.

application_fee.notes: Employment Pass application fee is SGD 105 according to MOM.
ICA charges an application fee of SGD 100 plus SGD 20 entry permit when permanent residency is approved.

estimated_processing_years.notes: ICA indicates that permanent residence applications may take up to six months to process.

annual_tuition_fee.notes: University Living notes that general master’s degrees in Singapore cost between SGD 20k and 45k per year; a midpoint of SGD 35k is used.

graduate_annual_salary.notes: TechTIQ Solutions reports entry‑level software engineers earning SGD 32k–75k; a midpoint of ~SGD 50k is used.

mid_level_annual_salary.notes: Mid‑level software engineers earn roughly SGD 105k–158k per year; a representative figure of SGD 130k is used.

senior_annual_salary.notes: Senior software engineers earn between SGD 158k and 237k; SGD 200k is a rounded mid‑range estimate.

annual_salary_growth_rate.notes: WTW survey indicates employers expect salary budgets to increase about 4% in 2026; used as salary growth assumption.

hourly_wage.notes: BCES article reports part‑time wages ranging SGD 8–20 per hour; SGD 12 is used as a typical rate.

annual_salary.notes: StashAway reports the median monthly income of full‑time employed residents was SGD 5,775 in 2025; annualised to ~SGD 69,300.

effective_income_tax_rate.notes: Singapore’s progressive tax system ranges from 0% to 24%. For incomes typical of this model, an effective rate of ~15% is assumed.

employer_superannuation_rate.notes: CPF employer contribution rate for employees aged 55 or below is 17% of wages from 1 Jan 2026.

single_monthly.notes: Numbeo estimates a single person’s monthly costs excluding rent at USD 1,152 (about SGD 1,489).

couple_monthly.notes: Couple general expenses approximated as twice the single person’s costs (2 × SGD 1,488 ≈ SGD 2,976).

family_with_one_child_monthly.notes: Family of four monthly costs excluding rent are about SGD 5,465; scaled to three people (~75%) to estimate a family with one child.

single_monthly.notes: Uhomes notes that a one‑bedroom apartment outside the city centre rents for about SGD 2,554/month in 2026.

family_monthly.notes: A three‑bedroom apartment outside the city centre averages SGD 4,695/month; converted at ~0.74 USD/SGD ≈ USD 3,473.

monthly_cost_per_child.notes: Numbeo reports private full‑day preschool or kindergarten fees of about SGD 1,829.79 per child per month.

public_transport_monthly.notes: Uhomes notes that a monthly public transport pass costs about SGD 128 in Singapore.

monthly_cost.notes: Uhomes suggests health insurance for students and expats ranges 40–200 SGD/month; an average value of 100 SGD is used.

used_car_purchase_price.notes: MoneySmart notes that a 5‑year‑old Toyota Corolla Altis costs around SGD 96,800–100,500; SGD 98,000 is used.

annual_insurance.notes: Great Eastern car ownership breakdown lists annual insurance cost of SGD 1,596.

annual_fuel.notes: Great Eastern notes petrol cost about SGD 3,295 per year (assuming 17,500 km and petrol price around SGD 2.90 per litre).

annual_maintenance.notes: Great Eastern breakdown lists maintenance cost of SGD 621 per year.

annual_depreciation_rate.notes: Typical depreciation for cars in Singapore is around 15% per year; assumption used because depreciation varies by model and age.

car_loan_interest_rate.notes: MoneySmart notes used car loans charge about 2.78%–3.0% per annum; a midpoint of 2.9% is used.

inflation_rate.notes: MAS and MTI maintained their headline and core inflation projections at 1.5%–2.5% for 2026; a midpoint of 2% is used.

savings_interest_rate.notes: Syfe’s June 2026 fixed‑deposit summary lists a 12‑month deposit rate of 1.60% p.a.; used as the assumed savings rate.

investment_return_rate.notes: SoFi article states that a good return on investment is around 7% per year; a conservative 5% annual return is assumed for the model.

sgd_to_lkr_exchange_rate.notes: OFX historical data shows average SGD to LKR rate ~248.975 for early 2026 and 259.96 on 25 Jun 2026; rounded to 1 SGD ≈ 250 LKR.

education_loan_interest_rate.notes: MOE/OCBC schedule shows standard education loan rates of 3% p.a. (Oct 2025–Mar 2026) and 2.6% p.a. (Apr 2026–Sep 2026); average ~2.8% is used.

migration_loan_interest_rate.notes: AsiaOne personal loan guide lists starting rates around 1.0%–1.8% with effective rates around 2%–3%; a 3% annual rate is used for migration loans.

loans_and_debt.notes: If cash becomes negative, convert it into explicit debt instead of silently allowing negative cash.

sources.notes: Store URLs or citation labels here.
